title: Nineteen Eighty-Four
subtitle: A Novel
author: George Orwell
authorBio: British journalist, novelist, essayist, and critic of totalitarianism. Died of tuberculosis at age 46, less than a year after 1984 was published.
editionNote: Plume (continuing reprint). First published in the UK by Secker & Warburg, 1949. ISBN 9780451524935 covers Signet's 1953 first US printing.
themes: The Fragility of the Human Spirit
excerpt: Published in 1949, George Orwell's final novel depicts a totalitarian future where the Party, led by the invisible Big Brother, enforces absolute power through perpetual war, omnipresent surveillance, deliberate historical falsification, and the systematic destruction of language via Newspeak. Winston Smith, a low-ranking member of the Outer Party, dares to rebel — through a forbidden love affair and acts of thoughtcrime — only to be crushed by the very power he sought to resist. A landmark of political fiction and the most influential warning about state overreach ever written.
